events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need to produce a JSON answer for this compliance check. The scenario: double-tapped breakers (non-listed). Panelboard installation & makeup, rough-complete stage. So we need to determine if it complies with code. Double-tapped breakers are generally not permitted unless the breaker is listed for two conductors. The NEC requires that conductors be terminated in accordance with their listing (110.3(B) and 110.14). Double-tapping a breaker not listed for two conductors is a violation. This is an industrial site, but still must follow code. So the work fails. Risk? Double-tapped breakers can cause overheating, loose connections, arcing, fire hazard. Could be high or critical. Since it's industrial, might be critical. I'll set decision: fail, risk: critical. Work stage: rough-complete. Component condition: installed-defective (since it's improperly installed) and non-compliant. Percent complete? It's roughed in, panelboard is installed and wired but not closed out. Maybe 80%? But double-tapping is a defect. I'll estimate 70%? Since it's roughed in, likely all breakers installed and wiring connected, so near 100% of installation, but defective. Percent complete would be progress of the scope, not quality. It might be close to complete, but with defects. So I'd say 80-90%. I'll put 85. Value: no value asked. Sound source: blank. Confidence: high. Event time: 0, rate: 0. Order: empty. Workable: maybe nothing, since it's fail, but if we need to list activities that can start? It says "when asked", so I'll leave empty array. Findings: "Double-tapped breakers not listed for two conductors", "Potential loose connection", "Non-compliant with NEC 110.3(B) and 110.14". Actions: "Replace breakers with listed two-conductor terminations or install additional breakers", "Torque connections to manufacturer specs", "Re-inspect". Rationale: "Non-listed double-tapped breakers violate NEC 110.3(B) and 110.14, creating a fire and shock hazard." References: ["NEC 110.3(B)", "NEC 110.14", "NEC Art. 408 (panelboard)"]. Also maybe 110.26 if clearance issues? Not mentioned. But double-tapping is not specific to 408 or 200.4 (neutral identification). I'll include those references. So final JSON.

This violates NEC 110.3(B) (equipment must be installed per listing), NEC 110.14(A) (terminals), and manufacturer instructions. Code references: NEC 110.3(B), 110.14(A).",
